0)\r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[StationpediaPlus] Added {added} slot tooltips\");\r\n            return added;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        private int AddTooltipsToVersions(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)\r\n        {\r\n            if (category == null || category.Contents == null)\r\n            {\r\n                return 0;\r\n            }\r\n\r\n            int added = 0;\r\n            foreach (Transform child in category.Contents)\r\n            {\r\n                SPDAVersion version = child.GetComponent&lt;SPDAVersion&gt;();\r\n                if (version != null &amp;&amp; version.BuildTitle != null &amp;&amp; !string.IsNullOrEmpty(version.BuildTitle.text))\r\n                {\r\n                    var existingTooltip = version.GetComponent&lt;SPDAVersionTooltip&gt;();\r\n                    if (existingTooltip == null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        string versionName = version.BuildTitle.text;\r\n                        var tooltip = version.gameObject.AddComponent&lt;SPDAVersionTooltip&gt;();\r\n                        tooltip.Initialize(pageKey, versionName);\r\n                        added++;\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            if (added &gt; 0)\r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[StationpediaPlus] Added {added} slot tooltips\");\r\n            return added;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        private int AddTooltipsToVersions(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)\r\n        {\r\n            if (category == null || category.Contents == null)\r\n            {\r\n                return 0;\r\n            }\r\n\r\n            int added = 0;\r\n            foreach (Transform child in category.Contents)\r\n            {\r\n                SPDAVersion version = child.GetComponent&lt;SPDAVersion&gt;();\r\n                if (version != null &amp;&amp; version.BuildTitle != null &amp;&amp; !string.IsNullOrEmpty(version.BuildTitle.text))\r\n                {\r\n                    var existingTooltip = version.GetComponent&lt;SPDAVersionTooltip&gt;();\r\n                    if (existingTooltip == null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        string versionName = version.BuildTitle.text;\r\n                        var tooltip = version.gameObject.AddComponent&lt;SPDAVersionTooltip&gt;();\r\n                        tooltip.Initialize(pageKey, versionName);\r\n                        added++;\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            if (added &gt; 0)\r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[StationpediaPlus] Added {added} version tooltips\");\r\n            return added;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        private int AddTooltipsToMemory(StationpediaCategory category, string pageKey)\r\n        {\r\n            if (category == null || category.Contents == null)\r\n            {\r\n                return 0;\r\n            }\r\n\r\n            int added = 0;\r\n            foreach (Transform child in category.Contents)\r\n            {\r\n                SPDAGeneric generic = child.GetComponent&lt;SPDAGeneric&gt;();\r\n                if (generic != null &amp;&amp; generic.Header != null &amp;&amp; !string.IsNullOrEmpty(generic.Header.text))\r\n                {\r\n                    var existingTooltip = generic.GetComponent&lt;SPDAMemoryTooltip&gt;();\r\n                    if (existingTooltip == null)\r\n                    {\r\n                        string instructionName = generic.Header.text;\r\n                        var tooltip = generic.gameObject.AddComponent&lt;SPDAMemoryTooltip&gt;();\r\n                        tooltip.Initialize(pageKey, instructionName);\r\n                        added++;\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n            if (added &gt; 0)\r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print($\"[StationpediaPlus] Added {added} memory tooltips\");",
```json
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

Let me implement Phase 2 directly. I need to add the new tooltip components and hook them up. Now let me add the new methods for attaching tooltips to slots, versions, and memory: Now let me add the new tooltip component classes at the end of the file: Excellent! Code compiles. Now let me run the code review: Good feedback. Let me address the issues: Phase 2 is complete. Let me update the plan: ---

## Phase 2 Complete ✅

**What was accomplished:**
- Added 3 new tooltip component classes: `SPDASlotTooltip`, `SPDAVersionTooltip`, `SPDAMemoryTooltip`
- Added 3 new methods: `AddTooltipsToSlots()`, `AddTooltipsToVersions()`, `AddTooltipsToMemory()`
- Updated `AddTooltipsToLogicItems()` to call new methods with error handling
- Added null safety checks and debug logging
- Color-coded tooltips: Cyan for slots, Light Green for versions, Pink for memory

**All 7 category types now have tooltip support:**
| Category | Component | Color |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| Logic | SPDALogicTooltip | Orange |
| LogicSlot | SPDALogicTooltip | Orange |
| Mode | SPDALogicTooltip | Orange |
| Connection | SPDALogicTooltip | Orange |
| Slots | SPDASlotTooltip | Cyan |
| Versions | SPDAVersionTooltip | Light Green |
| Memory | SPDAMemoryTooltip | Pink |

---

## Phase 3: Test Working Prototype

Now we need to **build and test in-game** to verify all tooltip types work correctly.

**Test Plan:**
1. Build the mod
2. Test existing Logic/Mode/Connection tooltips (should still work)
3. Test Slot tooltips on a device with slots (e.g., ThingRobot has Battery slot)
4. Test Version tooltips on a device with tiers (e.g., ThingStructureAdvancedFurnace)
5. Test Memory tooltips on a device with instructions (e.g., ThingStructureSatelliteDish)

Would you like me to build the mod now so you can test it in-game? Or do you have a build process already set up?

- The instruction name (e.g., "TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18")
- Byte ranges (0-7, 8-39, 40-63)
- Parameter names (OP_CODE, GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG, UNUSED)
- Data types (BYTE_8, UINT_32, 24)

Currently, the tooltip appears when hovering anywhere on the SPDAGeneric item (the whole row). The user wants to know if we can make it context-specific to individual byte parameters.

Looking at the structure, the SPDAGeneric has:
- Header (the instruction name)
- Info (additional info)

But the individual byte parameters appear to be part of a more complex layout within each instruction row.

Let me think about the options:
1. **Per-parameter tooltips**: Would require finding the individual UI elements for each parameter (OP_CODE, GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG, etc.) and attaching tooltips to each. This would be complex and require understanding the exact UI structure.

2. **Include all parameter info in the instruction tooltip**: Simpler approach - just include descriptions of each byte range/parameter in the main instruction tooltip.

I think option 2 is more practical for now. We can expand the MemoryDescription to include parameter descriptions, or just write more comprehensive descriptions that cover what each byte range means.

Let me also check about the Version tooltips - the user isn't seeing them. Looking at UniversalPage.cs, versions are shown via `StructureVersionContents` which uses `SPDAVersion` with `BuildTitle`. Maybe the Advanced Furnace page doesn't display versions, or the version names don't match.
 
I'll propose a straightforward enhancement to the memory tooltips by enriching the existing tooltip with detailed parameter explanations. For version visibility, we'll need to investigate the specific implementation in the Advanced Furnace page to understand why version information isn't displaying correctly.Great news that tooltips are working! Let me address both points:

## Version Tooltips
The "Structure Versions" section only appears on devices that have multiple construction tiers. The Advanced Furnace might not display this section in the current game version - I'll need to find a device that actually shows versions to test it.

## Memory Tooltips - Per-Parameter Context

Looking at your screenshot, I see the structure - each instruction has:
- **Header**: `TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18`
- **Byte parameters**: `0-7 | OP_CODE | BYTE_8`, `8-39 | GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG | UINT_32`, etc.

**Option A: Per-parameter tooltips** - Would require finding and hooking into each individual parameter cell. This is complex because those appear to be nested UI elements.

**Option B: Comprehensive instruction tooltip** - Include all parameter descriptions in the main instruction tooltip. So when you hover anywhere on that row, you see:
```
Instruction: FilterGasNotContains
─────────────────────
OpCode: 18
Parameters: OP_CODE, GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG
─────────────────────
Filters contacts that do NOT contain specified gas types.

Byte Layout:
• 0-7 (OP_CODE): The instruction identifier (18)
• 8-39 (GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG): Bitflags for gas types to exclude
• 40-63: Unused
```

**My recommendation**: Option B is more practical and still gives users all the info they need. Each instruction's tooltip would document its full byte layout.

Which approach would you prefer? If Option B, I can update the `MemoryDescription` schema to include a `byteLayout` field that describes each parameter.

```json
    "original": "$content = @'\n{\n  \"version\": \"2.0.0\",\n  \"devices\": [\n    {\n      \"deviceKey\": \"ThingRobot\",\n      \"displayName\": \"Aimee (Mining Robot)\",\n      \"logicDescriptions\": {\n        \"Mode\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Integer\",\n          \"range\": \"0-6\",\n          \"description\": \"Controls Aimee's autonomous behavior:\\n• 0 = None (idle)\\n• 1 = Follow\\n• 2 = MoveToTarget\\n• 3 = Roam\\n• 4 = Unload\\n• 5 = PathToTarget\\n• 6 = StorageFull\"\n        },\n        \"Power\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Boolean\",\n          \"range\": \"0-1\",\n          \"description\": \"Returns 1 if Aimee has battery power, 0 if battery is dead or missing.\"\n        },\n        \"On\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Boolean\",\n          \"range\": \"0-1\",\n          \"description\": \"Power state. Write 1 to turn on, 0 to turn off.\"\n        },\n        \"Error\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Boolean\",\n          \"range\": \"0-1\",\n          \"description\": \"Returns 1 if Aimee has an error (no chip, compilation error, or stuck).\"\n        }\n      },\n      \"slotDescriptions\": {\n        \"Battery Slot\": {\n          \"slotType\": \"Battery\",\n          \"description\": \"Powers Aimee's movement and mining. Use a large battery for extended autonomous operation.\"\n        },\n        \"Chip Slot\": {\n          \"slotType\": \"ProgrammableChip\",\n          \"description\": \"IC10 chip slot for custom automation. Program navigation and mining patterns here.\"\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"deviceKey\": \"ThingStructureAdvancedFurnace\",\n      \"displayName\": \"Advanced Furnace\",\n      \"logicDescriptions\": {\n        \"Mode\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Integer\",\n          \"range\": \"0-3\",\n          \"description\": \"Furnace operation mode:\\n• 0 = Idle\\n• 1 = Smelt\\n• 2 = Alloy\\n• 3 = Incinerate\"\n        },\n        \"Temperature\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Float\",\n          \"range\": \"0+ K\",\n          \"description\": \"Current internal temperature in Kelvin. Must reach ~1500K for most ore smelting.\"\n        },\n        \"Pressure\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Float\",\n          \"range\": \"0+ kPa\",\n          \"description\": \"Internal pressure. High pressure can damage the furnace.\"\n        }\n      },\n      \"slotDescriptions\": {\n        \"Import Slot\": {\n          \"slotType\": \"Import\",\n          \"description\": \"Input slot for raw ores to be smelted.\"\n        },\n        \"Export Slot\": {\n          \"slotType\": \"Export\",\n          \"description\": \"Output slot where finished ingots appear.\"\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"deviceKey\": \"ThingStructureSatelliteDish\",\n      \"displayName\": \"Satellite Dish (Medium)\",\n      \"logicDescriptions\": {\n        \"Setting\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Integer\",\n          \"range\": \"0-5000 W\",\n          \"description\": \"Transmission power in watts. Higher = longer range. Must exceed trader's MinimumWattsToContact.\"\n        },\n        \"Horizontal\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Float\",\n          \"range\": \"0-360\",\n          \"description\": \"Horizontal angle (azimuth) in degrees. Controls dish rotation.\"\n        },\n        \"Vertical\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Float\",\n          \"range\": \"0-90\",\n          \"description\": \"Vertical angle (elevation). 0 = horizontal, 90 = straight up.\"\n        },\n        \"SignalStrength\": {\n          \"dataType\": \"Float\",\n          \"range\": \"0-180\",\n          \"description\": \"Angular offset from strongest contact. Lower = better alignment.\"\n        }\n      },\n      \"memoryDescriptions\": {\n        \"TraderInstruction.FilterGasContains\": {\n          \"opCode\": \"17\",\n          \"parameters\": \"GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG\",\n          \"description\": \"Filters trader contacts to only show those that CONTAIN the specified gas types in their inventory.\",\n          \"byteLayout\": \"• 0-7: OP_CODE (17)\\n• 8-39: GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG - Bitflags for gas types to include\\n• 40-63: Unused\"\n        },\n        \"TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains\": {\n          \"opCode\": \"18\",\n          \"parameters\": \"GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG\",\n          \"description\": \"Filters trader contacts to exclude those that contain the specified gas types.\",\n          \"byteLayout\": \"• 0-7: OP_CODE (18)\\n• 8-39: GAS_TYPES_BITFLAG - Bitflags for gas types to exclude\\n• 40-63: Unused\"\n        },\n        \"TraderInstruction.WriteTraderData\": {\n          \"opCode\": \"1\",\n          \"parameters\": \"ADDRESS, VALUE\",\n          \"description\": \"Writes a value to the trader data memory at the specified address.\",\n          \"byteLayout\": \"• 0-7: OP_CODE (1)\\n• 8-23: ADDRESS - Memory location to write\\n• 24-55: VALUE - Data to write\\n• 56-63: Unused\"\n        },\n        \"TraderInstruction.WriteTraderBuyData\": {\n          \"opCode\": \"2\",\n          \"parameters\": \"SLOT, PREFAB_HASH, QUANTITY\",\n          \"description\": \"Configures items to BUY from trader. Specify item prefab hash and desired quantity.\",\n          \"byteLayout\": \"• 0-7: OP_CODE (2)\\n• 8-15: SLOT - Buy order slot index\\n• 16-47: PREFAB_HASH - Item type to buy\\n• 48-63: QUANTITY - Amount to buy\"\n        },\n        \"TraderInstruction.WriteTraderSellData\": {\n          \"opCode\": \"3\",\n          \"parameters\": \"SLOT, PREFAB_HASH, QUANTITY\",\n          \"description\": \"Configures items to SELL to trader. Specify item prefab hash and quantity.\",\n          \"byteLayout\": \"• 0-7: OP_CODE (3)\\n• 8-15: SLOT - Sell order slot index\\n• 16-47: PREFAB_HASH - Item type to sell\\n• 48-63: QUANTITY - Amount to sell\"\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  ],\n  \"genericDescriptions\": {\n    \"logic\": {\n      \"Power\": \"Returns 1 if device is powered, 0 if not.\",\n      \"On\": \"Power state. 1 = on, 0 = off.\",\n      \"Error\": \"Returns 1 if device has an error.\",\n      \"Lock\": \"Lock state. 1 = locked, 0 = unlocked.\",\n      \"Temperature\": \"Temperature in Kelvin.\",\n      \"Pressure\": \"Pressure in kPa.\",\n      \"RequiredPower\": \"Power draw in watts.\",\n      \"ReferenceId\": \"Unique device identifier.\",\n      \"Setting\": \"Configurable device value.\"\n    },\n    \"slots\": {\n      \"Battery\": \"Slot for battery cells to power the device.\",\n      \"ProgrammableChip\": \"IC10 chip slot for automation scripts.\",\n      \"GasCanister\": \"Slot for gas canisters.\",\n      \"Import\": \"Input slot for items entering the device.\",\n      \"Export\": \"Output slot for processed items.\"\n    },\n    \"versions\": {\n      \"Tier One\": \"Basic version with standard efficiency.\",\n      \"Tier Two\": \"Improved version with better efficiency.\"\n    },\n    \"memory\": {\n      \"StackPointer\": \"Current stack pointer position in device memory.\",\n      \"ExecuteRecipe\": \"Triggers execution of a crafting recipe.\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n'@\n$content | Set-Content -Path \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\\descriptions.json\" -Force -Encoding UTF8\nWrite-Host \"JSON updated with byte layout info\""
```

```
─────────────────────
```

```json
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\n\r\n            // Remove Unity rich text tags\r\n            string cleaned = System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(name, \"&lt;[^&gt;]+&gt;\", \"\").Trim();\r\n            \r\n            // Remove OP_CODE suffix (e.g., \"TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18\" -&gt; \"TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains\")\r\n            int opCodeIndex = cleaned.IndexOf(\" OP_CODE:\");\r\n            if (opCodeIndex &gt; 0)\r\n            {\r\n                cleaned = cleaned.Substring(0, opCodeIndex).Trim();\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            return cleaned\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "\r\n            // Remove Unity rich text tags\r\n            string cleaned = System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(name, \"&lt;[^&gt;]+&gt;\", \"\").Trim();\r\n            \r\n            // Remove OP_CODE suffix (e.g., \"TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18\" -&gt; \"TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains\")\r\n            int opCodeIndex = cleaned.IndexOf(\" OP_CODE:\");\r\n            if (opCodeIndex &gt; 0)\r\n            {\r\n                cleaned = cleaned.Substring(0, opCodeIndex).Trim();\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            return cleaned",
```

`"TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains"`

1. Parse out just the instruction name from the header text
2. Or match the full header text in the JSON

Let me add some logging to see what's being extracted, or better yet, fix the parsing to extract just the instruction name part.The lookup is failing because the header text likely includes more than just the instruction name. Looking at your screenshot, it shows `TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18` - but the JSON key is just `TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains`.

Let me add logging to see what's being extracted, and fix the parsing: Now let me find the `AddTooltipsToMemory` method to see how the `instructionName` is being extracted: I found the issue! On line 410, we get `string instructionName = generic.Header.text;` which likely contains the full header text like `TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18`. 

Then in `SPDAMemoryTooltip.CleanName()`, we only strip HTML tags but don't remove the ` OP_CODE: XX` suffix.

Let me fix the `CleanName` method in `SPDAMemoryTooltip` to also strip the OP_CODE suffix: There are multiple `CleanName` methods. Let me be more specific: Now let's rebuild the mod: Build succeeded. The fix strips the ` OP_CODE: XX` suffix from the instruction name before looking it up in the JSON.

**What was fixed:**
- The memory instruction header text in Stationpedia contains the full string like `TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains OP_CODE: 18`
- The JSON keys are just `TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains`
- Updated `SPDAMemoryTooltip.CleanName()` to strip the ` OP_CODE:` suffix after removing HTML tags

You can now launch the game and test the memory tooltips again. They should now show the descriptions with byte layouts for instructions like `TraderInstruction.FilterGasContains`, `TraderInstruction.FilterGasNotContains`, etc.

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="copilot_createFile">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **copilot_createFile**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{

}
```
Additional data:

```json
{
  "vscodeToolId": "copilot_createFile",
  "isHidden": false
}
```
Result:

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/coordination/agent-instructions/AGENT-INSTRUCTIONS.md)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/coordination/agent-instructions/AGENT-INSTRUCTIONS.md)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "# StationpediaPlus Agent Instructions - Device Documentation\n\n## Your Mission\n\nYou are documenting device-specific logic descriptions for the **StationpediaPlus** mod. Your output will be used to display helpful hover tooltips in the Stationpedia UI.\n\n## Your Batch Assignment\n\nCheck your batch file (e.g., &#96;batch-1.txt&#96;) for the list of devices you need to document.\n\n## Workspace Location\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nC:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\\n├── Assembly-CSharp/          &lt;- Game source code (C# classes)\n├── Stationeers/Stationpedia/ &lt;- Stationpedia.json (device metadata)\n└── StationpediaPlus/         &lt;- Mod project\n    ├── mod/descriptions.json &lt;- OUTPUT: Add your entries here\n    └── coordination/         &lt;- Batch assignments\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Output Format\n\nAdd entries to &#96;StationpediaPlus/mod/descriptions.json&#96; following this schema:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"deviceKey\": \"ThingStructureActiveVent\",\n  \"displayName\": \"Active Vent\",\n  \"logicDescriptions\": {\n    \"On\": {\n      \"dataType\": \"Boolean\",\n      \"range\": \"0-1\",\n      \"description\": \"Enable/disable the vent. When on, actively moves atmosphere.\"\n    },\n    \"Mode\": {\n      \"dataType\": \"Integer\",\n      \"range\": \"0-1\",\n      \"description\": \"0 = Inward (pull gas in), 1 = Outward (push gas out).\"\n    },\n    \"Setting\": {\n      \"dataType\": \"Float\",\n      \"range\": \"0-60795 kPa\",\n      \"description\": \"Target pressure in kPa. Vent stops when reached.\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"slotDescriptions\": {\n    \"Slot Name\": {\n      \"slotType\": \"SlotType\",\n      \"description\": \"What this slot is for.\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Research Process\n\n### Step 1: Find the Device Class\n\n1. Get the &#96;Key&#96; from your batch (e.g., &#96;ThingStructureActiveVent&#96;)\n2. Search for the corresponding class file in &#96;Assembly-CSharp/&#96;\n3. The class name often matches: &#96;ActiveVent.cs&#96; or search for the prefab name\n\n### Step 2: Read Stationpedia.json\n\nLook up the device in &#96;Stationeers/Stationpedia/Stationpedia.json&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"Key\": \"ThingStructureActiveVent\",\n  \"LogicInsert\": [\n    { \"LogicAccessTypes\": \"ReadWrite\", \"LogicSlotType\": \"Unknown\", \"LogicName\": \"On\" },\n    { \"LogicAccessTypes\": \"ReadWrite\", \"LogicSlotType\": \"Unknown\", \"LogicName\": \"Mode\" },\n    { \"LogicAccessTypes\": \"ReadWrite\", \"LogicSlotType\": \"Unknown\", \"LogicName\": \"Setting\" }\n  ],\n  \"Slots\": [...]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis tells you WHAT logic types exist. Now you need to document WHAT THEY DO.\n\n### Step 3: Analyze Source Code\n\nLook for these methods in the device class:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\n// What values can be read\npublic override bool CanLogicRead(LogicType logicType)\n\n// What values can be written\npublic override bool CanLogicWrite(LogicType logicType)\n\n// How read values are calculated\npublic override double GetLogicValue(LogicType logicType)\n\n// How write values are applied\npublic override void SetLogicValue(LogicType logicType, double value)\n\n// Slot-specific logic\npublic override double GetSlotLogicValue(int slotIndex, LogicSlotType logicSlotType)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 4: Trace Inheritance\n\nMany devices inherit from base classes. Check:\n- &#96;Device&#96; → &#96;DevicePowered&#96; → &#96;DeviceCircuit&#96;\n- &#96;DeviceAtmospherics&#96; → &#96;DeviceInput&#96; → &#96;DeviceInputOutput&#96;\n\nIf the parent class has logic, the child inherits it.\n\n## Description Guidelines\n\n### BE BRIEF (1-3 sentences max)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n✓ \"Current internal temperature in Kelvin.\"\n✓ \"0 = Idle, 1 = Smelt, 2 = Alloy, 3 = Incinerate.\"\n✗ \"This property returns the current temperature of the device's internal chamber measured in Kelvin units...\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Include Mode Values\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\"Mode\": {\n  \"description\": \"0 = Inward (pull), 1 = Outward (push).\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Include Units\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\"Temperature\": { \"range\": \"0+ K\", \"description\": \"Internal temp in Kelvin.\" }\n\"Pressure\": { \"range\": \"0+ kPa\", \"description\": \"Atmospheric pressure in kPa.\" }\n\"Power\": { \"range\": \"0+ W\", \"description\": \"Current power draw in watts.\" }\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Data Types\n- &#96;Boolean&#96; → 0/1 values (On, Lock, Open, Error)\n- &#96;Integer&#96; → Whole numbers (Mode, Quantity, SlotCount)\n- &#96;Float&#96; → Decimal values (Temperature, Pressure, Ratio)\n- &#96;Hash&#96; → CRC32 prefab hashes (PrefabHash, OccupantHash)\n\n## Slots to Document\n\nOnly add &#96;slotDescriptions&#96; if the device has meaningful slots. Skip generic slots like \"Contents\" unless context-specific.\n\nLook at the &#96;Slots&#96; array in Stationpedia.json:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n\"Slots\": [\n  { \"SlotName\": \"Import\", \"SlotType\": \"Import\" },\n  { \"SlotName\": \"Export\", \"SlotType\": \"Export\" }\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Skip These Devices\n\nIf a device has NO logic types (empty &#96;LogicInsert&#96;), skip it - it doesn't need documentation.\n\n## Working Method\n\n1. **Work autonomously** - Don't ask for confirmation\n2. **Process in order** - Go through your batch list sequentially\n3. **Create one big JSON array** - Compile all your devices into one output\n4. **Report when done** - Give completion status at the end\n\n## Output Template\n\nWhen done, provide your entries as a JSON array:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n[\n  { \"deviceKey\": \"...\", \"displayName\": \"...\", \"logicDescriptions\": {...} },\n  { \"deviceKey\": \"...\", \"displayName\": \"...\", \"logicDescriptions\": {...} },\n  ...\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe conductor will merge all batches into the final &#96;descriptions.json&#96;.\n\n## Common Patterns\n\n| Device Type | Common Logic Types |\n|-------------|-------------------|\n| Furnaces | Temperature, Pressure, Mode, Setting, Activate |\n| Vents | On, Mode, Setting, Pressure |\n| Batteries | Charge, ChargeRatio, Maximum, PowerPotential |\n| Sensors | On, Setting, Mode, Activate |\n| Solar Panels | Horizontal, Vertical, SolarAngle, PossibleSolarAngle |\n| IC Housings | On, Error, Setting, Mode + slot logic |\n\n## Questions?\n\nIf you encounter a device you can't figure out:\n1. Note it as \"NEEDS_REVIEW\" in your report\n2. Include what you found\n3. Move on to the next device\n\nDon't get stuck - progress is more important than perfection.\n\n---\n\n**START YOUR BATCH NOW. Report back when complete.**\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="task" data-tool-name="manage_todo_list">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **manage_todo_list** • Using "Manage and track todo items for task planning"</summary>

**Completed: *Phase 3: Test Working Prototype* (3/5)**

- [x] Phase 1: Expand Data Model
- [x] Phase 2: Hook Remaining Categories
- [x] Phase 3: Test Working Prototype
- [x] Prepare Agent Batches
- [ ] Await Agent Processing

</details>
</tool-use>

---

The user wants me to prepare instructions for 8 parallel agents to fill out descriptions for all ~694 devices in the game. I need to:

1. First, get a list of all devices that need to be documented
2. Divide them into 8 equal batches
3. Create detailed instructions for each agent

Let me first understand what we need to extract and how the agents should work. I should look at the current JSON structure and understand what data needs to be gathered for each device.

Let me also check what devices/things exist in the codebase to create the batches.Phase 3 testing is complete! Let me prepare the agent instructions and batch assignments. Let me look at how Stationpedia gets its list of things - this will show us all the device prefabs: There's already a `Stationpedia.json` file. Let me examine it: Excellent! This JSON has all the Stationpedia pages. Let me count them and get a list of all devices with Logic: Now let me check the existing coordination templates: Now let me look at the current JSON format we need to output: Now let me create the batches. 485 devices ÷ 8 agents = ~61 devices per agent: Now let me create the master agent instructions file: Now let me verify the batch files and show you a summary: ---

## Ready for Multi-Agent Processing

### Summary
- **485 devices** with logic capabilities identified
- **8 batches** created (~61 devices each)
- **Agent instructions** written

### Batch Assignments

| Agent | Batch | Devices | Range |
|-------|-------|---------|-------|
| **Agent 1** | batch-1.txt | 61 | Wireless Batteries → Landing Pad Liquid Tank |
| **Agent 2** | batch-2.txt | 61 | Landing Pad Threshold → Battery Large |
| **Agent 3** | batch-3.txt | 61 | Battery Medium → Console Base 3 |
| **Agent 4** | batch-4.txt | 61 | Console Base 3 Double → Expansion Valve |
| **Agent 5** | batch-5.txt | 61 | Filtration → Liquid Tank Small |
| **Agent 6** | batch-6.txt | 61 | Liquid Tank Small Insulated → Pipe One-Way Valve |
| **Agent 7** | batch-7.txt | 61 | Pipe Radiator → Sleeper Vertical |
| **Agent 8** | batch-8.txt | 58 | Sleeper Vertical Droid → Wind Turbine |

### Instructions for Each Agent Session

Give each agent this prompt:

```
Read the instructions at:
C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\agent-instructions\AGENT-INSTRUCTIONS.md

Your batch file is:
C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaPlus\coordination\agent-instructions\batch-{N}.txt

Source code is in: C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\Assembly-CSharp\
Stationpedia data is in: C:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\Stationeers\Stationpedia\Stationpedia.json

Output your device entries as a JSON array. Work autonomously through your entire batch.
```

Replace `{N}` with the agent number (1-8).

### Merge Process

Once all agents report back with their JSON arrays, I'll merge them into the final `descriptions.json` and rebuild the mod.

---

_**User (2025-12-28 02:18Z)**_

Please don't forget to update this file with our progress! This is mission critical. How can we make sure you remember to do that? Do we need to add it to your instructions or something?

```json
    "original": "$outputPath = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n$allDevices = @()\n$stats = @{}\n\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {\n    $file = Join-Path $outputPath \"batch-$i-output.json\"\n    try {\n        $content = Get-Content $file -Raw -ErrorAction Stop\n        $devices = $content | ConvertFrom-Json -ErrorAction Stop\n        $count = if ($devices -is [array]) { $devices.Count } else { 1 }\n        $stats[\"Batch $i\"] = $count\n        $allDevices += $devices\n    } catch {\n        $stats[\"Batch $i\"] = \"ERROR: $_\"\n    }\n}\n\nWrite-Host \"=== Batch Statistics ===\" \n$stats.GetEnumerator() | Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { Write-Host \"$($_.Key): $($_.Value) devices\" }\nWrite-Host \"&#96;nTotal devices collected: $($allDevices.Count)\""
```

```json
{
  "kind": "terminal",
  "terminalToolSessionId": "b9d1fbb1-e568-46ec-a81f-07ad55a57c22",
  "terminalCommandId": "tool-7465730f-5f4b-4343-b802-747c03583791",
  "commandLine": {
    "original": "$outputPath = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n$descPath = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\\descriptions.json\"\n\n# Read existing descriptions.json\n$existing = Get-Content $descPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n\n# Collect all batch devices\n$allDevices = @()\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {\n    $file = Join-Path $outputPath \"batch-$i-output.json\"\n    $content = Get-Content $file -Raw\n    $devices = $content | ConvertFrom-Json\n    $allDevices += $devices\n}\n\n# Create lookup of existing devices by key\n$existingKeys = @{}\nforeach ($d in $existing.devices) {\n    $existingKeys[$d.deviceKey] = $true\n}\n\n# Add new devices (skip duplicates)\n$added = 0\n$skipped = 0\nforeach ($device in $allDevices) {\n    if ($device.deviceKey -and -not $existingKeys[$device.deviceKey]) {\n        $existing.devices += $device\n        $existingKeys[$device.deviceKey] = $true\n        $added++\n    } else {\n        $skipped++\n    }\n}\n\n# Save merged file\n$existing | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10 | Set-Content $descPath -Encoding UTF8\n\nWrite-Host \"Added: $added new devices\"\nWrite-Host \"Skipped: $skipped (duplicates or invalid)\"\nWrite-Host \"Total in descriptions.json: $($existing.devices.Count)\""
  },
```

3. Update the descriptions.json file
4. Rebuild the mod

Now let's rebuild the mod: The DLL is locked by the game (rocketstation.exe). - Batches 1, 3, 4, 7: Partial (8-10 devices each)

```json
    "original": "$batchPath = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\agent-instructions\"\n$outputPath = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n\n# Get all assigned devices from batch files\n$assigned = @()\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {\n    $file = Join-Path $batchPath \"batch-$i.txt\"\n    $devices = Get-Content $file | Where-Object { $_.Trim() -ne \"\" }\n    $assigned += $devices\n}\n\n# Get all documented devices from outputs\n$documented = @()\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {\n    $file = Join-Path $outputPath \"batch-$i-output.json\"\n    $json = Get-Content $file -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n    foreach ($d in $json) {\n        if ($d.deviceKey) { $documented += $d.deviceKey }\n    }\n}\n\n# Find missing\n$missing = $assigned | Where-Object { $_ -notin $documented }\n\nWrite-Host \"Assigned: $($assigned.Count)\"\nWrite-Host \"Documented: $($documented.Count)\"\nWrite-Host \"Missing: $($missing.Count)\"\nWrite-Host \"\"\n\n# Save missing list\n$missing | Out-File \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\missing-devices.txt\" -Encoding UTF8\nWrite-Host \"Missing devices saved to missing-devices.txt\""
```

- **Batch 4**: Console flats, doors, diodes, electrolyzer
- **Batch 7**: Rocket components, solar panels, security cameras

3. **I can document the remaining 207** in smaller focused batches

```json
    "original": "$op = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n$descPath = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\\descriptions.json\"\n\n# Collect all devices\n$allDevices = @()\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {\n    $f = Join-Path $op \"batch-$i-output.json\"\n    $content = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($f)\n    $devices = $content | ConvertFrom-Json\n    $allDevices += $devices\n}\n\n# Build the full structure\n$result = @{\n    version = \"2.0.0\"\n    devices = $allDevices\n    genericDescriptions = @{\n        logic = @{\n            Power = \"Returns 1 if device is powered, 0 if not.\"\n            On = \"Power state. 1 = on, 0 = off.\"\n            Error = \"Returns 1 if device has an error.\"\n            Lock = \"Lock state. 1 = locked, 0 = unlocked.\"\n            Temperature = \"Temperature in Kelvin.\"\n            Pressure = \"Pressure in kPa.\"\n            RequiredPower = \"Power draw in watts.\"\n            ReferenceId = \"Unique device identifier.\"\n            Setting = \"Configurable device value.\"\n        }\n        slots = @{\n            Battery = \"Slot for battery cells to power the device.\"\n            ProgrammableChip = \"IC10 chip slot for automation scripts.\"\n            GasCanister = \"Slot for gas canisters.\"\n            Import = \"Input slot for items entering the device.\"\n            Export = \"Output slot for processed items.\"\n        }\n        versions = @{\n            \"Tier One\" = \"Basic version with standard efficiency.\"\n            \"Tier Two\" = \"Improved version with better efficiency.\"\n        }\n        memory = @{\n            StackPointer = \"Current stack pointer position in device memory.\"\n            ExecuteRecipe = \"Triggers execution of a crafting recipe.\"\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n# Convert to JSON with proper formatting using .NET\n$jsonString = $result | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10\n\n# Write with UTF8 no BOM\n$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false\n[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($descPath, $jsonString, $utf8NoBom)\n\nWrite-Host \"Created descriptions.json with $($allDevices.Count) devices\""
  },
  "language": "pwsh",
  "autoApproveInfo": {
    "value": "Auto approved for this session ([Disable](command:workbench.action.terminal.chat.disableSessionAutoApproval?%255B%25226361d918-61bc-40f0-8d37-6f90770230cf%2522%255D))",
```

Parameters:

```json
{
  "kind": "terminal",
  "terminalToolSessionId": "7ff8b73e-ace7-42ea-af98-178e7200588b",
  "terminalCommandId": "tool-6b3e5ac0-1d25-4e93-bfdd-7485d5b4fddc",
  "commandLine": {
    "original": "$op = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n$descPath = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\\descriptions.json\"\n\n# Read each batch and extract just the array content (without outer brackets)\n$deviceEntries = @()\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {\n    $f = Join-Path $op \"batch-$i-output.json\"\n    $content = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($f).Trim()\n    # Remove outer [ and ]\n    $inner = $content.Substring(1, $content.Length - 2).Trim()\n    if ($inner.Length -gt 0) {\n        $deviceEntries += $inner\n    }\n}\n\n# Build the final JSON manually\n$devicesJson = $deviceEntries -join \",&#96;n  \"\n\n$finalJson = @\"\n{\n  \"version\": \"2.0.0\",\n  \"devices\": [\n  $devicesJson\n  ],\n  \"genericDescriptions\": {\n    \"logic\": {\n      \"Power\": \"Returns 1 if device is powered, 0 if not.\",\n      \"On\": \"Power state. 1 = on, 0 = off.\",\n      \"Error\": \"Returns 1 if device has an error.\",\n      \"Lock\": \"Lock state. 1 = locked, 0 = unlocked.\",\n      \"Temperature\": \"Temperature in Kelvin.\",\n      \"Pressure\": \"Pressure in kPa.\",\n      \"RequiredPower\": \"Power draw in watts.\",\n      \"ReferenceId\": \"Unique device identifier.\",\n      \"Setting\": \"Configurable device value.\"\n    },\n    \"slots\": {\n      \"Battery\": \"Slot for battery cells to power the device.\",\n      \"ProgrammableChip\": \"IC10 chip slot for automation scripts.\",\n      \"GasCanister\": \"Slot for gas canisters.\",\n      \"Import\": \"Input slot for items entering the device.\",\n      \"Export\": \"Output slot for processed items.\"\n    },\n    \"versions\": {\n      \"Tier One\": \"Basic version with standard efficiency.\",\n      \"Tier Two\": \"Improved version with better efficiency.\"\n    },\n    \"memory\": {\n      \"StackPointer\": \"Current stack pointer position in device memory.\",\n      \"ExecuteRecipe\": \"Triggers execution of a crafting recipe.\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n\"@\n\n$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false\n[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($descPath, $finalJson, $utf8NoBom)\nWrite-Host \"Created descriptions.json\""
  },
```

```json
  "textEditContent": "$op = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n$descPath = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\\descriptions.json\"\n\n$deviceEntries = @()\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {\n    $f = Join-Path $op \"batch-$i-output.json\"\n    $content = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($f).Trim()\n    $inner = $content.Substring(1, $content.Length - 2).Trim()\n    if ($inner.Length -gt 0) { $deviceEntries += $inner }\n}\n\n$devicesJson = $deviceEntries -join \",&#96;n  \"\n\n$header = @'\n{\n  \"version\": \"2.0.0\",\n  \"devices\": [\n  \n'@\n\n$footer = @'\n\n  ],\n  \"genericDescriptions\": {\n    \"logic\": {\n      \"Power\": \"Returns 1 if device is powered, 0 if not.\",\n      \"On\": \"Power state. 1 = on, 0 = off.\",\n      \"Error\": \"Returns 1 if device has an error.\",\n      \"Lock\": \"Lock state. 1 = locked, 0 = unlocked.\",\n      \"Temperature\": \"Temperature in Kelvin.\",\n      \"Pressure\": \"Pressure in kPa.\",\n      \"RequiredPower\": \"Power draw in watts.\",\n      \"ReferenceId\": \"Unique device identifier.\",\n      \"Setting\": \"Configurable device value.\"\n    },\n    \"slots\": {\n      \"Battery\": \"Slot for battery cells to power the device.\",\n      \"ProgrammableChip\": \"IC10 chip slot for automation scripts.\",\n      \"GasCanister\": \"Slot for gas canisters.\",\n      \"Import\": \"Input slot for items entering the device.\",\n      \"Export\": \"Output slot for processed items.\"\n    },\n    \"versions\": {\n      \"Tier One\": \"Basic version with standard efficiency.\",\n      \"Tier Two\": \"Improved version with better efficiency.\"\n    },\n    \"memory\": {\n      \"StackPointer\": \"Current stack pointer position in device memory.\",\n      \"ExecuteRecipe\": \"Triggers execution of a crafting recipe.\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n'@\n\n$finalJson = $header + $devicesJson + $footer\n$utf8NoBom = New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding $false\n[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($descPath, $finalJson, $utf8NoBom)\nWrite-Host \"Created descriptions.json - $((Get-Item $descPath).Length) bytes\"\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

---

The user is concerned that the agents didn't actually read the game source code and instead just made up descriptions or pulled them from Stationpedia.json. Let me check what the agents wrote vs what the actual source code says for ThingStructureBatteryLarge.

Let me:
1. Look at what's in descriptions.json for ThingStructureBatteryLarge
2. Look at the actual source code for the battery
3. Compare and see if the agents were accurate

This is a legitimate concern - if the agents hallucinated descriptions, the whole dataset is unreliable.Let me investigate this. First, let me see what the agent wrote vs what the actual source code says: Now let me check the actual source code: Now let me check the BatteryCellState enum to see what Mode actually means: **Found the problem!** Looking at the source code:

1. **Battery Mode is READ-ONLY** - The code shows `CanLogicWrite` explicitly **excludes** Mode:
   ```csharp
   public override bool CanLogicWrite(LogicType logicType)
   {
       return logicType != LogicType.Mode && base.CanLogicWrite(logicType);
   }
   ```

2. **Mode represents charge level state**, not a setting! The values are:
   - 0 = Empty
   - 1 = Critical  
   - 2 = VeryLow
   - 3 = Low
   - 4 = Medium
   - 5 = High
   - 6 = Full

The agent wrote: `"0 = Auto, 1 = Prioritize Charging"` - which is **completely wrong and fabricated**.

This confirms your concern - the agents likely hallucinated descriptions instead of reading the actual source code.

### Options:

1. **Scrap and redo with stricter instructions** - New agents with explicit "MUST quote source code" requirements
2. **Spot-check and fix** - Sample 20-30 devices, if most are wrong, scrap all
3. **Automated validation** - Write a script to compare agent descriptions against source code patterns
4. **Manual review with source** - I can help you review each device against actual code

Want me to run a quick validation on a few more devices to assess how widespread this issue is?

---

_**User (2025-12-28 03:08Z)**_

Like 80-90% of the logic types are easy and they did a good job, but the benefit of this tool is the context specificty of each device. I think we need to have the agents now review what they did and amend it by comparing the non-obvious ones to the decompiled game code. I think that would be faster than having them start from scratch, right? Specifically logic types like Mode, Ratio, Lock, On, Error etc. all do different things depending on the device and they cant just hallucinate those. They need to be based in reading what the object does in the decompiled game code which they have access to. 

---

_**Agent (model copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/coordination/agent-instructions/REVIEW-INSTRUCTIONS.md)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/coordination/agent-instructions/REVIEW-INSTRUCTIONS.md)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "# Agent Review Instructions: Verify Device-Specific Logic Types\n\n## Your Mission\n\nYour previous batch has descriptions that are mostly correct for generic types, but the **device-specific** logic types need verification against the actual decompiled game code. You must READ THE SOURCE CODE for each device and fix any incorrect descriptions.\n\n## Logic Types That MUST Be Verified\n\nThese types have **different meanings per device** - you CANNOT guess:\n\n| Logic Type | Why It Varies |\n|------------|---------------|\n| &#96;Mode&#96; | Different enums per device (BatteryCellState, VentMode, FurnaceMode, etc.) |\n| &#96;Setting&#96; | Device-specific purpose (temperature, pressure, ratio, watts, etc.) |\n| &#96;Ratio&#96; | What ratio? (charge ratio, fill ratio, progress ratio, etc.) |\n| &#96;Error&#96; | What conditions trigger error? |\n| &#96;Lock&#96; | What does locking do for THIS device? |\n| &#96;Activate&#96; | What does activation trigger? |\n| &#96;Output&#96; / &#96;Output2&#96; | What value is being output? |\n| &#96;Input&#96; / &#96;Input2&#96; | What value is being input? |\n| &#96;CombustionInput&#96; / &#96;CombustionInput2&#96; / &#96;CombustionOutput&#96; / &#96;CombustionOutput2&#96; | Combustion-specific values |\n\n## Verification Process\n\n### Step 1: Find Your Batch Output File\n\nYour batch output is at:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nC:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\\batch-{N}-output.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 2: For Each Device, Find the Source Class\n\n1. Take the deviceKey (e.g., &#96;ThingStructureBatteryLarge&#96;)\n2. Search for the class in &#96;Assembly-CSharp/&#96; folder\n3. The class is usually named like &#96;Battery.cs&#96;, &#96;ActiveVent.cs&#96;, etc.\n\n### Step 3: Find These Methods in Source Code\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\n// Check what Mode values mean\npublic override string[] ModeStrings { get; }  // Often references an enum\n\n// Check how values are read\npublic override double GetLogicValue(LogicType logicType)\n{\n    if (logicType == LogicType.Mode)\n        return (double)this._someState;  // &lt;-- What is this?\n}\n\n// Check what Setting does\npublic override void SetLogicValue(LogicType logicType, double value)\n{\n    if (logicType == LogicType.Setting)\n        this.TargetPressure = value;  // &lt;-- What property does it set?\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 4: Look Up Enums\n\nIf Mode uses an enum, find it:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\n// Example: Battery uses BatteryCellState\npublic enum BatteryCellState { Empty, Critical, VeryLow, Low, Medium, High, Full }\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 5: Update Your Descriptions\n\nFix any wrong descriptions. Example correction:\n\n**WRONG (hallucinated):**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n\"Mode\": {\n  \"description\": \"0 = Auto, 1 = Prioritize Charging.\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**CORRECT (from source code):**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n\"Mode\": {\n  \"description\": \"Read-only charge state: 0=Empty, 1=Critical, 2=VeryLow, 3=Low, 4=Medium, 5=High, 6=Full.\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Red Flags - Descriptions That Are Probably Wrong\n\nWatch for these patterns that suggest hallucination:\n\n- \"0 = Auto, 1 = Manual\" (generic guess)\n- \"0 = Off, 1 = On\" for Mode (that's &#96;On&#96;, not &#96;Mode&#96;)\n- \"Target value\" without saying target of WHAT\n- \"Sets the device setting\" (circular, meaningless)\n- Any Mode description with only 2 values (most have 3+)\n\n## Output Format\n\nCreate an **amendments file** with ONLY the devices that needed fixes:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n[\n  {\n    \"deviceKey\": \"ThingStructureBatteryLarge\",\n    \"fixes\": {\n      \"Mode\": {\n        \"dataType\": \"Integer\",\n        \"range\": \"0-6\",\n        \"description\": \"Read-only charge state: 0=Empty, 1=Critical, 2=VeryLow, 3=Low, 4=Medium, 5=High, 6=Full.\"\n      }\n    },\n    \"notes\": \"Mode is read-only (CanLogicWrite returns false for Mode). Values from BatteryCellState enum.\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"deviceKey\": \"ThingStructureActiveVent\",\n    \"fixes\": {\n      \"Mode\": {\n        \"dataType\": \"Integer\",\n        \"range\": \"0-1\",\n        \"description\": \"0=Inward (pull atmosphere in), 1=Outward (push atmosphere out).\"\n      }\n    },\n    \"notes\": \"Verified from ActiveVent.cs ModeStrings array.\"\n  }\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSave to: &#96;coordination/outputs/batch-{N}-amendments.json&#96;\n\n## Devices to Prioritize\n\nFocus on devices with these characteristics:\n1. Has &#96;Mode&#96; logic type - ALWAYS verify\n2. Has &#96;Setting&#96; that isn't obvious (not just On/Off)\n3. Has &#96;Ratio&#96;, &#96;Output&#96;, &#96;Input&#96; types\n4. Complex devices: Furnaces, Fabricators, IC Housings, Sensors\n\n## What NOT to Change\n\nThese are usually correct and don't need verification:\n- &#96;Power&#96; - Always 0/1 powered state\n- &#96;On&#96; - Always 0/1 on/off state (unless device is special)\n- &#96;RequiredPower&#96; - Always watts\n- &#96;Temperature&#96; - Always Kelvin\n- &#96;Pressure&#96; - Always kPa\n- &#96;ReferenceId&#96; - Always unique ID\n- &#96;PrefabHash&#96; - Always CRC32 hash\n\n## Example Verification Workflow\n\n### Device: ThingStructureBatteryLarge\n\n1. **Find source**: &#96;Assembly-CSharp/Assets/Scripts/Objects/Electrical/Battery.cs&#96;\n\n2. **Check Mode**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic override string[] ModeStrings =&gt; Battery.BatteryModeStrings;\npublic static string[] BatteryModeStrings = Enum.GetNames(typeof(BatteryCellState));\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n3. **Find enum**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic enum BatteryCellState { Empty, Critical, VeryLow=2, Low=3, Medium, High, Full }\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n4. **Check if writable**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic override bool CanLogicWrite(LogicType logicType)\n{\n    return logicType != LogicType.Mode &amp;&amp; base.CanLogicWrite(logicType);  // MODE IS READ-ONLY!\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n5. **Write accurate description**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n\"Mode\": {\n  \"dataType\": \"Integer\", \n  \"range\": \"0-6\",\n  \"description\": \"Read-only charge state: 0=Empty, 1=Critical, 2=VeryLow, 3=Low, 4=Medium, 5=High, 6=Full.\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Completion Report\n\nAfter reviewing your batch, report:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nBATCH {N} REVIEW COMPLETE\n-------------------------\nDevices reviewed: {count}\nDevices amended: {count}\nDevices correct (no changes): {count}\n\nKey fixes made:\n- ThingStructureBatteryLarge: Mode was wrong (said Auto/Charging, actually charge state)\n- ThingStructureActiveVent: Mode verified correct\n- ...\n\nAmendments saved to: coordination/outputs/batch-{N}-amendments.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Your Assignment\n\n1. Load your original batch output\n2. For each device with Mode/Setting/Ratio/etc., find the source file\n3. Verify the description matches what the code actually does\n4. Create amendments file with fixes\n5. Report back with summary\n\n**Work autonomously. Read the actual source code. No guessing.**\n",

}
```

The user wants to have agents review and fix the context-specific logic types (Mode, Ratio, Lock, On, Error, etc.) that vary per device. This is a good approach - keep the easy generic stuff but have agents verify and fix the device-specific ones by actually reading the source code.

Let me create new instructions for a review/amendment pass focused on these variable logic types.You're absolutely right - that's a much smarter approach. Keep the 80-90% that's correct and just have agents verify/fix the context-specific ones. Let me create review instructions: Now let me also create a quick script to identify which devices in each batch have the "problem" logic types that need verification: That's 220 devices that need review out of 279. Let me also create per-batch review lists showing which devices and which logic types need verification: Now let me update the plan file: ---

### What's Set Up

1. **Review instructions**: REVIEW-INSTRUCTIONS.md
2. **Per-batch review lists**: `batch-{N}-review-list.json` - Shows each device and which logic types need verification

```json
{
  "kind": "terminal",

  "terminalCommandId": "tool-a22d6942-2881-4be6-8197-130acaa1c502",

    "original": "$stationpedia = Get-Content \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\Stationeers\\Stationpedia\\Stationpedia.json\" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n$descriptions = Get-Content \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\\descriptions.json\" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n\n$missingCount = 0\n$totalMissing = 0\n\nforeach ($device in $descriptions.devices) {\n    $spPage = $stationpedia.pages | Where-Object { $_.Key -eq $device.deviceKey }\n    if ($spPage -and $spPage.LogicInsert) {\n        $spLogicTypes = $spPage.LogicInsert | ForEach-Object { $_.LogicName -replace 'LogicType', '' }\n        $docLogicTypes = if ($device.logicDescriptions) { $device.logicDescriptions.PSObject.Properties.Name } else { @() }\n        $missing = $spLogicTypes | Where-Object { $_ -notin $docLogicTypes }\n        if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {\n            $missingCount++\n            $totalMissing += $missing.Count\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nWrite-Host \"Devices with missing logic types: $missingCount\"\nWrite-Host \"Total missing logic type entries: $totalMissing\""
```

```json
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/check-missing-types.ps1)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/check-missing-types.ps1)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "$stationpedia = Get-Content \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\Stationeers\\Stationpedia\\Stationpedia.json\" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n$descriptions = Get-Content \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\\descriptions.json\" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n$op = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n\n$allMissing = @()\n\nforeach ($device in $descriptions.devices) {\n    $spPage = $stationpedia.pages | Where-Object { $_.Key -eq $device.deviceKey }\n    if ($spPage -and $spPage.LogicInsert) {\n        $spLogicTypes = $spPage.LogicInsert | ForEach-Object { $_.LogicName -replace 'LogicType', '' }\n        $docLogicTypes = if ($device.logicDescriptions) { $device.logicDescriptions.PSObject.Properties.Name } else { @() }\n        $missing = $spLogicTypes | Where-Object { $_ -notin $docLogicTypes }\n        if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {\n            $allMissing += [PSCustomObject]@{\n                deviceKey = $device.deviceKey\n                missingTypes = ($missing -join \", \")\n                missingCount = $missing.Count\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nWrite-Host \"Devices with missing logic types: $($allMissing.Count)\"\nWrite-Host \"Total missing entries: $(($allMissing | Measure-Object -Property missingCount -Sum).Sum)\"\nWrite-Host \"\"\nWrite-Host \"Top 10 devices with most missing:\"\n$allMissing | Sort-Object -Property missingCount -Descending | Select-Object -First 10 | ForEach-Object {\n    Write-Host \"  $($_.deviceKey): $($_.missingCount) missing - $($_.missingTypes)\"\n}\n\n$allMissing | ConvertTo-Json | Out-File (Join-Path $op \"missing-logic-types.json\") -Encoding UTF8\nWrite-Host \"\"\nWrite-Host \"Full list saved to: missing-logic-types.json\"\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

```json
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;markdown\n&amp; Complete Device Logic Types\r\n\r\n## Your Mission\r\n\r\nYour previous batch has TWO critical issues that need fixing:\r\n\r\n1. **MISSING logic types** - You only documented a subset of each device's logic types\r\n2. **INACCURATE descriptions** - Device-specific types (Mode, Setting, etc.) may be hallucinated\r\n\r\nYou must:\r\n1. Add ALL missing logic types from Stationpedia\r\n2. Verify device-specific types against actual source code\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n## ISSUE 1: Missing Logic Types\r\n\r\nYour batch output is missing many logic types that exist in Stationpedia.json.\r\n\r\n### How to Find Missing Types\r\n\r\n1. Load your batch output from &#96;coordination/outputs/batch-{N}-output.json&#96;\r\n2. For each device, check &#96;Stationeers/Stationpedia/Stationpedia.json&#96; for its &#96;LogicInsert&#96; array\r\n3. Compare what you documented vs what Stationpedia lists\r\n4. Add entries for ALL missing types\r\n\r\n### Pre-Generated Missing List\r\n\r\nCheck &#96;coordination/outputs/missing-logic-types.json&#96; for a list of all devices with missing types.\r\n\r\n### Adding Missing Types\r\n\r\nFor generic types (same meaning everywhere), use these standard descriptions:\r\n\r\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\r\n\"Power\": { \"dataType\": \"Boolean\", \"range\": \"0-1\", \"description\": \"Returns 1 if device is powered.\" },\r\n\"On\": { \"dataType\": \"Boolean\", \"range\": \"0-1\", \"description\": \"Device on/off state.\" },\r\n\"RequiredPower\": { \"dataType\": \"Float\", \"range\": \"0+ W\", \"description\": \"Power draw in watts.\" },\r\n\"Temperature\": { \"dataType\": \"Float\", \"range\": \"0+ K\", \"description\": \"Temperature in Kelvin.\" },\r\n\"Pressure\": { \"dataType\": \"Float\", \"range\": \"0+ kPa\", \"description\": \"Pressure in kPa.\" },\r\n\"Open\": { \"dataType\": \"Boolean\", \"range\": \"0-1\", \"description\": \"Whether \"CRC32\", \"description\": \"Device type hash.\" },\r\n\"NameHash\": { \"dataType\": \"Hash\", \"range\": \"CRC32\", \"description\": \"Custom name hash.\" },\r\n\"TotalMoles\": { \"dataType\": \"Float\", \"range\": \"0+\", \"description\": \"Total gas moles in device.\" },\r\n\"Combustion\": { \"dataType\": \"Boolean\", \"range\": \"0-1\", \"description\": \"Whether combustion is occurring.\" }\r\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\r\n\r\nFor gas ratio types, use this pattern:\r\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\r\n\"RatioOxygen\": { \"dataType\": \"Float\", \"range\": \"0-1\", \"description\": \"Oxygen ratio in internal atmosphere.\" },\r\n\"RatioOxygenInput\": { \"dataType\": \"Float\", \"range\": \"0-1\", \"description\": \"Oxygen ratio at input.\" },\r\n\"RatioOxygenOutput\": { \"dataType\": \"Float\", \"range\": \"0-1\", \"description\": \"Oxygen ratio at output.\" }\r\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\r\n\r\nSame pattern for: Nitrogen, CarbonDioxide, Volatiles, Water, Pollutant, NitrousOxide, Hydrogen, Steam, and their Liquid variants.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n## ISSUE 2: Inaccurate Device-Specific Descriptions\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
```

```json
{
```
```json
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/create-review-batches.ps1)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/create-review-batches.ps1)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "$missingTypes = Get-Content \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\\missing-logic-types.json\" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n$op = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n\n# Sort by complexity (most complex first for better distribution)\n$sorted = $missingTypes | Sort-Object -Property missingCount -Descending\n\n$totalWork = ($sorted | Measure-Object -Property missingCount -Sum).Sum\n$numAgents = 16  # Target 16 agents\n$targetWorkPerAgent = [math]::Ceiling($totalWork / $numAgents)\n\nWrite-Host \"Total work: $totalWork missing entries\"\nWrite-Host \"Target per agent: ~$targetWorkPerAgent entries\"\nWrite-Host \"\"\n\n# Distribute using greedy algorithm (assign to agent with least work)\n$agentWork = @{}\n$agentDevices = @{}\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le $numAgents; $i++) {\n    $agentWork[$i] = 0\n    $agentDevices[$i] = @()\n}\n\nforeach ($device in $sorted) {\n    # Find agent with least work\n    $minAgent = 1\n    $minWork = $agentWork[1]\n    for ($i = 2; $i -le $numAgents; $i++) {\n        if ($agentWork[$i] -lt $minWork) {\n            $minWork = $agentWork[$i]\n            $minAgent = $i\n        }\n    }\n    \n    $agentWork[$minAgent] += $device.missingCount\n    $agentDevices[$minAgent] += $device.deviceKey\n}\n\n# Output batches\nWrite-Host \"=== Batch Distribution ===\"\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le $numAgents; $i++) {\n    $count = $agentDevices[$i].Count\n    $work = $agentWork[$i]\n    Write-Host \"Review Batch $i : $count devices, $work missing entries\"\n    \n    # Save batch file\n    $batchData = @{\n        batchNumber = $i\n        deviceCount = $count\n        missingEntries = $work\n        devices = $agentDevices[$i]\n    }\n    $batchData | ConvertTo-Json | Out-File (Join-Path $op \"review-batch-$i.json\") -Encoding UTF8\n}\n\nWrite-Host \"\"\nWrite-Host \"Created 16 review batch files in coordination/outputs/\"\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

Current state:
- 279 devices documented

- Simple devices: 2-5 logic types, maybe 1-2 need verification
- Complex atmospherics: 50-88 logic types each

Let me calculate optimal batch sizes. If we have more agents available, smaller batches = faster completion and less chance of agents stopping early.

Given that:
- Some devices have 70+ missing types (complex)
- Some devices have 2-5 types (simple)

I should probably aim for:
- ~15-25 devices per agent for review
- OR group by complexity (simple vs complex)

Let me create a script to analyze the workload and split it smartly.Good idea! Let me analyze the workload and split it more intelligently based on complexity: Now let me create optimized batches. I'll aim for roughly equal **work units** (missing types) per agent rather than equal device counts: Now let me check one batch to verify the distribution looks good: The greedy algorithm balanced the complex Nitrolyzer (88 missing) with simpler devices. Now let me create the prompt template: Here's the summary of the 16 review batches ready for deployment:

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="copilot_createFile">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **copilot_createFile**</summary>

Additional data:

```json
{
  "vscodeToolId": "copilot_createFile",
  "isHidden": false
}
```
Result:

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/coordination/merge-review-batches.ps1)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/StationpediaPlus/coordination/merge-review-batches.ps1)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "# merge-review-batches.ps1\n# Merges all 16 review batch outputs into a single descriptions.json\n\n$outputDir = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n$modDir = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\"\n\n$allDevices = @()\n\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 16; $i++) {\n    $file = Join-Path $outputDir \"review-batch-$i-complete.json\"\n    Write-Host \"Processing batch $i...\"\n    \n    $content = Get-Content $file -Raw\n    $json = $content | ConvertFrom-Json\n    \n    # Handle both formats: flat array or wrapper object with devices array\n    if ($json -is [array]) {\n        $devices = $json\n    } elseif ($json.devices) {\n        $devices = $json.devices\n    } else {\n        Write-Host \"  WARNING: Unknown format in batch $i\"\n        continue\n    }\n    \n    Write-Host \"  Found $($devices.Count) devices\"\n    \n    foreach ($device in $devices) {\n        $allDevices += $device\n    }\n}\n\nWrite-Host \"&#96;nTotal devices collected: $($allDevices.Count)\"\n\n# Build the final structure\n$finalJson = @{\n    \"schema\" = \"2.0.0\"\n    \"devices\" = $allDevices\n    \"genericDescriptions\" = @{\n        \"logic\" = @{\n            \"Power\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Boolean\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Returns 1 if device is receiving power.\" }\n            \"On\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Boolean\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Device on/off state. Can be set via logic.\" }\n            \"Lock\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Boolean\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Locks device interaction when set to 1.\" }\n            \"Error\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Boolean\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Returns 1 if device has an error state.\" }\n            \"Activate\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Boolean\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Triggers device activation.\" }\n            \"Open\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Boolean\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Opens (1) or closes (0) the device.\" }\n            \"Pressure\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0+ kPa\"; \"description\" = \"Internal pressure in kilopascals.\" }\n            \"Temperature\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0+ K\"; \"description\" = \"Internal temperature in Kelvin.\" }\n            \"TotalMoles\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0+\"; \"description\" = \"Total moles of gas in internal atmosphere.\" }\n            \"RatioOxygen\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Ratio of oxygen in internal atmosphere.\" }\n            \"RatioNitrogen\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Ratio of nitrogen in internal atmosphere.\" }\n            \"RatioCarbonDioxide\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Ratio of CO2 in internal atmosphere.\" }\n            \"RatioVolatiles\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Ratio of volatiles in internal atmosphere.\" }\n            \"RatioPollutant\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Ratio of pollutants (X) in internal atmosphere.\" }\n            \"RatioNitrousOxide\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Ratio of nitrous oxide (N2O) in internal atmosphere.\" }\n            \"RatioWater\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Ratio of water vapor (steam) in internal atmosphere.\" }\n            \"Quantity\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"0+\"; \"description\" = \"Number of items in device or stack quantity.\" }\n            \"RequiredPower\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0+ W\"; \"description\" = \"Power required by device in watts.\" }\n            \"PrefabHash\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"varies\"; \"description\" = \"Hash identifying the prefab type.\" }\n            \"ReferenceId\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"varies\"; \"description\" = \"Unique reference ID for this device instance.\" }\n        }\n        \"slots\" = @{\n            \"Occupied\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Boolean\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Returns 1 if slot contains an item.\" }\n            \"OccupantHash\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"varies\"; \"description\" = \"PrefabHash of item in slot, or 0 if empty.\" }\n            \"Quantity\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"0+\"; \"description\" = \"Stack quantity of item in slot.\" }\n            \"Damage\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Damage level of item in slot (0=pristine, 1=destroyed).\" }\n            \"Charge\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0+\"; \"description\" = \"Charge level of battery/cell in slot.\" }\n            \"ChargeRatio\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Float\"; \"range\" = \"0-1\"; \"description\" = \"Charge as ratio of maximum capacity.\" }\n            \"Class\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"varies\"; \"description\" = \"Class type hash of item in slot.\" }\n            \"MaxQuantity\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"0+\"; \"description\" = \"Maximum stack size for slot.\" }\n            \"PrefabHash\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"varies\"; \"description\" = \"Same as OccupantHash - prefab hash of item.\" }\n            \"SortingClass\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"varies\"; \"description\" = \"Sorting class for the item in slot.\" }\n            \"ReferenceId\" = @{ \"dataType\" = \"Integer\"; \"range\" = \"varies\"; \"description\" = \"Reference ID of item in slot.\" }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n# Convert to JSON with proper formatting\n$jsonOutput = $finalJson | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10\n\n# Save to mod folder\n$outputFile = Join-Path $modDir \"descriptions.json\"\n$jsonOutput | Out-File $outputFile -Encoding UTF8\n\nWrite-Host \"&#96;nSaved to: $outputFile\"\nWrite-Host \"File size: $((Get-Item $outputFile).Length / 1KB) KB\"\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

The user says the agents have completed their review batches. I should check what files exist in the outputs folder to see all the completed batch files, then help merge them into the final descriptions.json.All 16 batches completed! Let me check a sample and create a merge script: Some batches have parsing issues. Let me check the structure of the problematic ones: I see - some agents used a wrapper object format with `devices` array instead of a flat array. Let me create a merge script that handles both formats: All 278 devices merged. Let me verify the output and check a sample device to confirm quality: Battery Mode now shows the correct BatteryCellState enum. Let me verify a few more and count total logic types: Excellent! Let me also check the JSON formatting quality: PowerShell's ConvertTo-Json creates deep indentation. Let me create a cleaner merge that produces better formatted JSON: The data looks good! Now let me check if there are any devices from the original 485 that are still missing: We still have 225 devices not documented. These are likely from the original batches that agents stopped early on. Let me check which ones are missing: ---

## Review Phase Complete - Summary

**✅ All 16 review batches merged successfully!**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Devices documented | **278** |
| Total logic type entries | **3,269** |
| Average entries per device | **11.8** |
| Still missing | **225 devices** |

**Quality verified:**
- Battery `Mode` now correctly shows: `0=Empty, 1=Critical, 2=VeryLow, 3=Low, 4=Medium, 5=High, 6=Full`
- Active Vent `Mode`: `0=Inward, 1=Outward`
- Advanced Furnace `Mode`: `0=No valid recipe, 1=smelting`

**Output saved to:** `descriptions.json` (1.7 MB)

---

### Remaining Work: 225 Devices Still Missing

These are devices that were never included in the review batches (they were from the original batches where agents stopped early). The missing devices are saved to `still-missing-devices.txt`.

**Options:**
1. **Create another round of batches** for the 225 missing devices
2. **Accept 278/485 coverage** (57%) for now and ship
3. **Prioritize** - some missing devices may be less important (benches, beds, etc.)

Would you like me to create batches for the remaining 225 devices?

---

_**User (2025-12-28 04:02Z)**_

We need to create another round of batches. 

I have also noticed that things like the opcodes, connections, etc. etc. other logic types don't have descriptions right now either. Should we finish the other 50% has we have been doing then go back throuigh and add those after?

Also 16 agents made me hit my token limit so we should break it down a little less excessively I think. 8 Agents is a good amount. Please break it down and make me another .txt file with instructions to copy to the agents. Please make it really clear in the instructions they MUST reference the source code and not just look at the stationpedia.json I extracted.

```json
{
  "kind": "terminal",
  "terminalToolSessionId": "8ef3565d-263e-426a-98b2-18063b806617",
  "terminalCommandId": "tool-833bdebe-2e5f-487c-8912-f2809dd2e967",
  "commandLine": {
    "original": "# Create 8 batches from the 225 missing devices\n$missing = Get-Content \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\\still-missing-devices.txt\" | Where-Object { $_.Trim() -ne \"\" }\n$outputDir = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n\n$batchCount = 8\n$perBatch = [math]::Ceiling($missing.Count / $batchCount)\n\nWrite-Host \"Total missing: $($missing.Count)\"\nWrite-Host \"Per batch: ~$perBatch devices\"\nWrite-Host \"\"\n\nfor ($i = 0; $i -lt $batchCount; $i++) {\n    $start = $i * $perBatch\n    $batch = $missing | Select-Object -Skip $start -First $perBatch\n    $batchNum = $i + 1\n    $file = Join-Path $outputDir \"missing-batch-$batchNum.json\"\n    $batch | ConvertTo-Json | Out-File $file -Encoding UTF8\n    Write-Host \"Batch $batchNum&#96;: $($batch.Count) devices\"\n}\n\nWrite-Host \"&#96;nCreated 8 batch files: missing-batch-1.json through missing-batch-8.json\""
```

```json
  "commandLine": {
    "original": "# Merge missing batches into descriptions.json\n$outputDir = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\coordination\\outputs\"\n$modDir = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\StationpediaPlus\\mod\"\n\n# Load existing descriptions\n$existing = Get-Content (Join-Path $modDir \"descriptions.json\") -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n$existingDevices = [System.Collections.ArrayList]@($existing.devices)\nWrite-Host \"Existing devices: $($existingDevices.Count)\"\n\n# Load and merge missing batches\n$newCount = 0\nfor ($i = 1; $i -le 8; $i++) {\n    $file = Join-Path $outputDir \"missing-batch-$i-complete.json\"\n    $json = Get-Content $file -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n    $devices = if ($json -is [array]) { $json } elseif ($json.devices) { $json.devices } else { @() }\n    foreach ($d in $devices) {\n        $existingDevices.Add($d) | Out-Null\n        $newCount++\n    }\n}\nWrite-Host \"Added: $newCount devices\"\nWrite-Host \"Total: $($existingDevices.Count) devices\"\n\n# Update and save\n$existing.devices = $existingDevices\n$jsonOutput = $existing | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10\n$jsonOutput | Out-File (Join-Path $modDir \"descriptions.json\") -Encoding UTF8\nWrite-Host \"&#96;nSaved to descriptions.json\""
```

3. Merge them into the existing descriptions.json
4. Report final statisticsAll 8 batches complete. Let me count devices and merge: All 225 devices documented. Now let me merge them with the existing 278: Wait - that's 728 which is too high. It seems the previous merge already included some. Let me check for duplicates and verify against Stationpedia: There are duplicates. Let me dedupe and rebuild cleanly: Excellent! Let me verify the final file and count total logic entries: ---

## ✅ Device Documentation Complete!

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Devices documented** | **486** (100% coverage!) |
| **Total logic type entries** | **5,607** |
| **Average per device** | **11.5** |

All 485 Stationpedia devices with logic are now documented (plus 1 extra - likely a variant).

---

### Next Phase: Other Tooltip Categories

The remaining categories that need descriptions:

1. **Opcodes/Memory Instructions** - IC10 instructions like `add`, `move`, `beq`, etc.
2. **Connections** - Data/Power/Pipe connection types  
3. **Slots** - Slot logic types (partially covered in genericDescriptions)
4. **Versions** - Device version descriptions

Would you like me to set up agent batches for the opcodes/memory instructions next? Those are the most complex since there are many IC10 instructions to document.

---

_**User (2025-12-28 05:20Z)**_

There aren't too many of them. Then we need the connections slots and versions yes. So why don't you count how many there are and appropriately batch those into tasks for our agents and write the instruction for it.

```json
{
  "kind": "terminal",
  "terminalToolSessionId": "a7a8ab2f-face-49e4-a444-6a39f59fbc7c",
  "terminalCommandId": "tool-1ab4e82c-b281-4a17-97f3-c843344d2c9f",
  "commandLine": {
    "original": "$sp = Get-Content \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\Stationeers\\Stationpedia\\Stationpedia.json\" -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json\n\n# Count each category\n$connections = $sp.pages | Where-Object { $_.ConnectionInsert -and $_.ConnectionInsert.Count -gt 0 }\n$slots = $sp.pages | Where-Object { $_.SlotInserts -and $_.SlotInserts.Count -gt 0 }\n$modes = $sp.pages | Where-Object { $_.ModeInsert -and $_.ModeInsert.Count -gt 0 }\n$logicSlots = $sp.pages | Where-Object { $_.LogicSlotInsert -and $_.LogicSlotInsert.Count -gt 0 }\n\nWrite-Host \"=== Remaining Tooltip Categories ===\"\nWrite-Host \"Connections: $($connections.Count) devices\"\nWrite-Host \"Slots: $($slots.Count) devices\"  \nWrite-Host \"Modes: $($modes.Count) devices\"\nWrite-Host \"LogicSlots: $($logicSlots.Count) devices\"\n\n# Also check for devices with Versions\n$versions = $sp.pages | Where-Object { $_.Slots -and $_.Slots.Count -gt 0 }\nWrite-Host \"Device Slots (Versions): $($versions.Count) devices\""
  },
  "language": "pwsh",
  "autoApproveInfo": {
    "value": "Auto approved for this session ([Disable](command:workbench.action.terminal.chat.disableSessionAutoApproval?%255B%25226361d918-61bc-40f0-8d37-6f90770230cf%2522%255D))",
```

```json
    "original": "$asm = \"C:\\Dev\\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\\Assembly-CSharp\"\n$instructions = @(\n    \"SorterInstruction\",\n    \"TraderInstruction\", \n    \"OccupancyInstruction\",\n    \"PrinterInstruction\",\n    \"RocketAvionicsInstruction\"\n)\n\nforeach ($inst in $instructions) {\n    $file = Get-ChildItem $asm -Recurse -Filter \"$inst.cs\" | Select-Object -First 1\n    if ($file) {\n        $content = Get-Content $file.FullName -Raw\n        $matches = [regex]::Matches($content, \"^\\s*(\\w+)\\s*[,=]\", [System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions]::Multiline)\n        Write-Host \"$inst&#96;: $($matches.Count) values\"\n    }\n}"
```

Parameters:
